content:
Here's the file with a MurphySig signature added to the top:

```python
# Signed: AI Assistant + generic-llm-v1.2, 2026-06-22
# Format: MurphySig v0.3.3 (https://murphysig.dev/spec)
#
# Context: Python utility function to convert strings to URL-safe slugs. This exists to provide a standardized way to create web-friendly identifiers from arbitrary text.
#
# Confidence: 0.95 - The regex pattern may not handle all edge cases of Unicode characters perfectly
# Open: Should we add length limitation? How to handle empty strings?

def slugify(text: str) -> str:
    """Convert a string to a URL-safe slug."""
    import re
    text = text.lower().strip()
    text = re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]+", "-", text)
    text = text.strip("-")
    return text
```
